content:
PANEL REVIEW: MOAT Specification v0.2.0 Changes

You are reviewing the changes made to the MOAT specification in v0.2.0. These changes implement the trust anchor design decisions that this panel previously approved. Your job is to verify the spec text accurately reflects the design intent, catches edge cases, and maintains spec quality.

CHANGES MADE:
1. Section 2 rewritten into 3 subsections: 2.1 (who implements MOAT — platform-first framing), 2.2 (four-link trust chain narrative), 2.3 (specification overview)
2. Three new fields: publisher_identity (6.3.15, CONDITIONAL), repository_owner_id (6.3.16, RECOMMENDED), sigstore_trust_root (6.3.17, OPTIONAL)
3. publisher_identity and repository_owner_id added to Section 8.1 hashed fields allowlist. sigstore_trust_root explicitly excluded (distribution metadata).
4. Section 9.2 updated with steps 6 (source_repo binding via Fulcio OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.57264.1.12) and 7 (publisher_identity check), plus TOFU note. Forgejo removed from supported platforms.
5. Three new security subsections: 11.17 (source binding residual risks), 11.18 (version rollback WARNING), 11.19 (first-publish trust / TOFU)
6. Section 11.1 trust model updated to include source binding as link 4. Section 11.3 updated to note source_repo is no longer purely self-reported with Sigstore.
7. Appendix C Forgejo entry corrected.
8. New Appendix D: Provider OIDC certificate extensions (D.1), enterprise self-hosted Sigstore (D.2), sigstore-a2a related work (D.3)
9. TV-MH4: new test vector for publisher_identity + repository_owner_id in meta hash
10. Version bumped to 0.2.0, date to 2026-04-03

REVIEW CRITERIA:
- Does the spec text accurately reflect the panel's design decisions?
- Are there normative gaps — places where the text should say MUST/SHOULD/MAY but doesn't, or vice versa?
- Are there ambiguities that would cause two independent implementations to behave differently?
- Are there internal contradictions between sections?
- Are edge cases handled?
- Is the spec over-specifying anything (baking in policy that should be consumer-decided)?

CRITICAL: C-1 SSH has no source binding equivalent — 9.2 step 6 is Sigstore-only but spec gives no normative guidance for SSH-signed public content. C-2 publisher_identity lies-by-omission — verifiers can't detect when the field should have been present but wasn't; 'differs' definition is in informative Note. MAJOR: M-1 sigstore_trust_root has no normative consumer behavior for unknown roots. M-2 No mention of Fulcio certificate expiry — implementers will reject all content as expired. M-3 sigstore_trust_root excluded from hash means registries can swap it undetected. M-4 Scope promotion guidance is informative, not normative. M-5 Step 5 identity verification is MUST with no testable criteria. MINOR: m-1 repository_owner_id extraction from OID URI not specified. m-2 log_index tamper risk not documented. m-3 Forgejo correction language too absolute. m-4 TOFU MUST in 11.19 contradicts informative note in 9.2. m-5 published_at YAML parsing for non-conforming files unaddressed. NOTE: N-1 TV-MH4 source_repo has 4 segments but 6.3.8 defines host/owner/repo (3 segments). N-2 D.3 SHOULD coordination with sigstore-a2a is unactionable. N-3 No convention for latest-version manifest format. N-4 No JSON Schema for meta.yaml validation.

CRITICAL: ES-C1 Step 5 identity verification is MUST but unconfigured policy = undefined compliance posture. ES-C2 sigstore_trust_root has no normative participation in verification procedure (9.2). MAJOR: ES-M1 'Surface to user' in step 7 undefined for headless CI/CD. ES-M2 'Differs' definition is in informative Note, violates panel consensus requiring normative definition. ES-M3 Repository transfer section (11.17) lacks verifier SHOULD that exists in 11.19. ES-M4 'Signed latest-version manifest' SHOULD has no specified mechanism. ES-M5 Enterprise Sigstore path (D.2) is informative but has normative dependencies. MINOR: ES-mn1 Rekor inclusion proof SHOULD undersells requirement for strict consumers. ES-mn2 publisher_identity missing YAML boolean coercion note. ES-mn3 Custom domain / reverse proxy case for self-hosted not addressed. ES-mn4 Scope table publisher_identity condition doesn't reference 6.3.15.

MAJOR: SP-1 publisher_identity conditionality requires crypto knowledge to evaluate — solo devs won't know if it applies. SP-2 repository_owner_id has no acquisition path for non-Sigstore publishers. MINOR: SP-3 sigstore_trust_root absent from 6.4 scope table. SP-4 Step 7 gives no guidance on HOW to surface publisher_identity. SP-5 sigstore_trust_root role is ambiguous (publisher advertisement vs consumer policy). NOTE: SP-6 Section 2.1 creates false expectation — says users don't manage crypto, then 20 sections of crypto. SP-7 TV-MH4 source_repo has 4+ path components but 6.3.8 only defines host/owner/repo.

CRITICAL: RO-1 publisher_identity is free-form unverified string presented as trust signal — no verification mechanism. MAJOR: RO-2 sigstore_trust_root excluded from hash enables CDN-layer trust root substitution. RO-3 TOFU first-publish policy completely deferred — not reflected in 5.3 registry conformance. RO-4 No canonical content ID composition rule — registries will fragment on namespacing. RO-5 Source binding undefined for non-GitHub/GitLab OIDC — two-tier behavior not normatively stated. MINOR: RO-6 'Signed latest-version manifest' SHOULD is unimplementable without format guidance. RO-7 repository_owner_id RECOMMENDED means no systematic coverage for account resurrection. RO-8 No registry ingestion guidance for delegated publishing. NOTE: RO-9 D.3 SHOULD coordination with sigstore-a2a is aspirational.

CRITICAL: SPu-1 publisher_identity REQUIRED trigger is unverifiable by consumers — publisher-only obligation but spec implies verification. MAJOR: SPu-2 Step 5 MUST with no testable algorithm — phantom normative requirement. SPu-3 sigstore_trust_root has conflicting meta.yaml vs registry-manifest semantics. SPu-4 sigstore_trust_root missing from 6.4 scope table. MINOR: SPu-5 Trust chain link 4 is a verifier-side policy check, not a structural link — Section 2.2 overstates dependency. SPu-6 'No override' in 5.2.1 contradicts informative UX note. SPu-7 RECOMMENDED header in 6.3.16 inconsistent with other field conventions. SPu-8 Operational mitigations in normative security section 11.17. SPu-9 MUST in 11.19 applied to determination implementations can't make ('first publish'). SPu-10 RFC 2119 SHOULD in D.3 applied to third-party spec. NOTE: SPu-11 sigstore_trust_root as un-hinted field opens trust root substitution. SPu-12 TV-MH4 source_repo path depth inconsistent with 6.3.8. SPu-13 Strict consumer source binding obligation not mirrored in 5.2.2.

ROUND 1 SUMMARY — 5 panelists, 48 total findings.

CONSENSUS ISSUES (raised by 3+ panelists):
1. publisher_identity verifiability gap — PV, ES, RO, SPu all flag that verifiers can't detect absent-when-required. RO adds it's an unverified free-form string.
2. sigstore_trust_root semantics — PV, ES, RO, SPu flag no normative verification behavior, hash exclusion enables substitution, conflicting meta.yaml vs registry-manifest usage.
3. Step 5 identity verification phantom MUST — PV, ES, SPu flag MUST with no testable criteria.
4. TV-MH4 / 6.3.8 source_repo format — PV, SP, SPu flag 4-segment path inconsistent with host/owner/repo definition.
5. 'Differs' definition in informative Note — PV, ES flag this violates consensus requiring normative definition.
6. sigstore_trust_root absent from 6.4 scope table — SP, SPu flag this.
7. Signed latest-version manifest SHOULD without mechanism — PV, ES, RO flag.
8. TOFU first-publish MUST untestable — SPu, PV flag.

ROUND 2 FOCUS: Panelists respond to each other's findings. For consensus issues 1-8, state whether you agree, disagree, or have a counter-proposal. Maximum 5 findings per panelist — focus on the highest-impact items.

1. AGREE + counter-propose structured URI for publisher_identity. 2. AGREE + split into per-package and per-registry fields. 3. AGREE demote to SHOULD. 4. AGREE fix test vector or update 6.3.8. 5. AGREE move to normative. 6. AGREE add row. 7. AGREE either specify format or downgrade to NOTE. 8. AGREE demote to registry SHOULD. Also elevate: ES-M1 headless CI, RO-4 canonical content ID, SPu-10 remove RFC 2119 from D.3.

1. AGREE strengthen with SAN binding. 2. AGREE + counter-propose signed trust-root-binding assertion. 3. AGREE demote to SHOULD + require documented algorithm. 4. AGREE fix to 3 segments. 5. AGREE promote to normative. 6. AGREE add row. 7. AGREE either specify or remove SHOULD. 8. AGREE move to 5.3 registry conformance. Also elevate: RO-2 CDN substitution, RO-3 TOFU in 5.3, SPu-8 operational mitigations.

1. AGREE add explicit 'self-reported unverified' language. 2. AGREE add normative consumer MUST for trust root usage. 3. AGREE demote to SHOULD with example. 4. AGREE fix test vector or update definition. 5. AGREE move to normative. 6. AGREE add row. 7. AGREE downgrade to MAY or specify mechanism. 8. AGREE move to registries. Also elevate: PV-M2 Fulcio cert expiry, ES-M1 headless CI, RO-4 content ID.

1. AGREE + counter-propose URI format requirement. 2. AGREE + reconsider hash exclusion as security parameter. 3. AGREE downgrade to SHOULD. 4. AGREE spec defect. 5. AGREE move to normative. 6. AGREE add row. 7. AGREE specify or drop to NOTE. 8. AGREE + counter-propose registry-side first-seen-binding invariant in 5.3. Also elevate: RO-4 canonical content ID, ES-M1 headless CI, SPu-13 strict conformance mirror.

1. AGREE root fix is making trigger condition testable. 2. AGREE + counter-propose pick one semantic (publisher-advertised or consumer-local). 3. AGREE demote to SHOULD with strict-consumer documentation MUST. 4. AGREE fix contradiction. 5. AGREE promote with precise comparison semantics. 6. AGREE add row. 7. AGREE specify minimum or remove SHOULD. 8. AGREE reclassify as registry SHOULD. Also elevate: ES-M3 transfer lacks verifier SHOULD, RO-5 non-GitHub/GitLab two-tier, PV-M2 Fulcio cert expiry.

ROUND 2 SUMMARY — Full 5-0 consensus on all 8 issues. Actionable fixes identified.

1. publisher_identity: 5-0 AGREE the field is unverifiable as written. Split on fix: URI format (PV, RO) vs explicit 'self-reported unverified' label (SP, SPu) vs SAN binding (ES). Minimum viable fix: add normative text stating the field is self-reported, MUST NOT be treated as verified identity. URI format is a v2 consideration.

2. sigstore_trust_root: 5-0 AGREE normative verification behavior is missing. Fix: add SHOULD to 9.2 for trust root usage when field is present; add security note that field is not integrity-protected.

3. Step 5 phantom MUST: 5-0 AGREE demote to SHOULD. Add: strict consumers MUST document their identity verification algorithm.

4. TV-MH4 source_repo: 5-0 AGREE fix needed. Simplest: fix TV-MH4 to use 3-segment path. If platform monorepo paths are needed, update 6.3.8.

5. 'Differs' definition: 5-0 AGREE move to normative text.

6. sigstore_trust_root in 6.4: 5-0 AGREE add row.

7. Signed latest-version manifest: 5-0 AGREE current SHOULD is unimplementable. Fix: downgrade to non-normative recommendation (remove SHOULD, use 'registries are encouraged to').

8. TOFU MUST: 5-0 AGREE demote from verifier MUST to registry-side SHOULD in 5.3.

ADDITIONAL FIXES WITH 3+ VOTES:
- PV-M2 Fulcio certificate expiry guidance (PV, SP, SPu) — add to 9.2
- ES-M1 headless CI 'surface to user' (ES, SP, RO, PV) — clarify step 7
- RO-4 canonical content ID (PV, SP, RO) — non-normative recommendation
- SPu-13 strict consumer source binding in 5.2.2 (RO, SPu) — mirror 9.2 requirement
- RO-5 non-GitHub/GitLab OID absence (SPu, RO) — normative behavior when OID absent
